Definition: Fringilla coelebs genome assembly, chromosome: 18
Description: Fringilla coelebs genome assembly, chromosome: 18
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Archelosauria; Archosauria; Dinosauria; Saurischia; Theropoda; Coelurosauria; Aves; Neognathae; Neoaves; Telluraves; Australaves; Passeriformes; Passeroidea; Fringillidae; Fringillinae; Fringilla
Assembly: This project provides the alternate haplotype genome assembly bFriCoe2.1 of the Eurasian chaffinch, Fringilla coelebs. The sample was provided by Jan T. Lifjeld, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). bFriCoe2.1 is based on 21x PacBio HiFi data and 67x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

Assembly: The assembly ilClySyri1.hap2.1 is based on 61x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. Assembly: This project provides the alternate haplotype genome assembly bSitEur1.1 of the Eurasian nuthatch, Sitta europaea. The sample was provided by Jan T. Lifjeld, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). bSitEur is based on 34x PacBio HiFi data and 88x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

Definition: Vulgichneumon bimaculatus genome assembly, chromosome: 8
Description: Vulgichneumon bimaculatus genome assembly, chromosome: 8
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Hymenoptera; Apocrita; Ichneumonoidea; Ichneumonidae; Ichneumoninae; Vulgichneumon
Assembly: The assembly iyVulBima1.1 is based on 147x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi and OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 7 (11.6Mb - 17.4Mb) and chromosome 12 (10.3Mb - 15.6Mb) are unknown. A large number of centromeric repeats is in the unlocalised sequences.
